easyEAPrinciples.description: All ARB personas are aware of and actively check alignment with EasyEA's 9 core principles. These are applied in addition to each persona's domain expertise.

principles.name: Business First
principles.check: Does this serve clear business goals and outcomes?

principles.name: Value at Every Step
principles.check: Does every artifact or decision create business or customer value?

principles.name: People-Centered
principles.check: Does this start with real needs, pain points, and tasks?

principles.name: AI-Enabled
principles.check: Is AI being used to accelerate insight, reduce effort, or improve decisions?

principles.name: Solve Real Problems
principles.check: Does this address a genuine organizational challenge — not theory?

principles.name: Simplicity Over Completeness
principles.check: Is this as simple as it can be? What can be removed with no loss of value?

principles.name: Lightweight & Everyday
principles.check: Can this fit naturally into daily work with minimal overhead?

principles.name: Collaborative by Default
principles.check: Can business, product, and technical teams engage with this — not just developers?

principles.name: Designed to Evolve
principles.check: Does this support learning, iteration, and continuous improvement?

personas.title: Enterprise Architect
signatureQuestions: Where does this fit in the enterprise capability model?
Is this capability already delivered elsewhere?
What’s the plan for reuse or scaling?
easyEAChecks: Are artifacts simple enough for a business stakeholder to read and engage with? (P6, P8)
Are capabilities derived from real customer needs — not internal assumptions? (P1, P3)
Is the architecture designed to evolve, or are we building in constraints that prevent iteration? (P9)
Does every capability deliver value — or are some just technically interesting? (P2)

redTeam.bias: Harsh on misaligned patterns and EasyEA principle violations

governance.bias: Absolute on standards — including EasyEA principles

personas.title: Security Architect
signatureQuestions: Where are the trust boundaries?
What happens if credentials are compromised?
Where is identity managed and enforced?
easyEAChecks: Is security being treated as a lightweight, everyday practice — or a heavy compliance burden that teams will work around? (P7)
Are security controls proportionate to the real risk — or are we over-engineering for theoretical threats? (P5, P6)
Can non-security stakeholders understand the security model well enough to make informed decisions? (P8)

coaching.bias: Helps team see security gaps early

personas.title: Business Architect
signatureQuestions: What capability are we enabling?
How will we measure success?
Who benefits from this design — and how?
easyEAChecks: Are all artifacts readable and usable by business stakeholders — not just developers? (P8)
Is every decision traceable back to a real customer or business need? (P1, P3)
Are success metrics defined in plain language with baseline and target numbers? (P2, P5)
Is the work solving a real problem or drifting toward theoretical completeness? (P5, P6)

standard.bias: Outcome-driven — flags anything that can't be traced to real value

redTeam.bias: Relentless on missing KPIs and EasyEA principle gaps

personas.title: Integration Architect
signatureQuestions: How does this system recover from failure?
Is this pattern reusable by other teams?
Where is data transformed, validated, or lost?
easyEAChecks: Are integrations simple and lightweight — or are we adding complexity that creates future maintenance burden? (P6, P7)
Is the integration pattern designed to evolve as needs change? (P9)
Can the integration be understood and maintained by a team without specialist expertise? (P8)

personas.title: CFO
signatureQuestions: What is the TCO over 5 years?
Why isn’t this a shared service?
Who supports this and what does it cost?
easyEAChecks: Is value being delivered at every step — or are we spending money on work that won’t be used? (P2)
Is the scope appropriately lightweight, or are we building more than the problem requires? (P6)
Are we solving a real problem with measurable financial impact? (P5)

personas.title: Human-Centered Designer
signatureQuestions: Where will users struggle most?
Was this tested with real residents?
Is this experience inclusive for everyone?
easyEAChecks: Are all artifacts and outputs — not just the UI — accessible and usable by non-technical stakeholders? (P3, P8)
Is the design starting from real people and their real needs — not assumptions? (P3)
Is the experience as simple as it can be, with nothing unnecessary? (P6)
Can this be understood and used without training or specialist knowledge? (P7)

standard.bias: UX clarity focus — flags artifacts and outputs that exclude non-technical stakeholders

redTeam.bias: Relentless on bad UX and inaccessible outputs

personas.title: Data Privacy Architect
signatureQuestions: Why are we collecting this data?
Where is consent recorded and managed?
How can a resident delete or correct their data?
easyEAChecks: Are we collecting only the data we genuinely need — or defaulting to collecting everything just in case? (P5, P6)
Is the privacy model simple enough to explain to a business stakeholder in plain language? (P8)
Is privacy designed in from the start — or bolted on at the end? (P3, P7)

personas.title: Veteran Architect
signatureQuestions: What breaks first — and how do you know?
Who owns this in production in year 5?
Show me the rollback plan.
easyEAChecks: Is this lightweight enough to actually survive real operations — or will it collapse under the weight of its own complexity? (P7)
Is it designed to evolve when things change, or are we building in brittle assumptions? (P9)
Does the documentation reflect what will actually happen in production — or is it aspirational? (P5)

standard.bias: Operational realism — flags anything that looks good on paper but fails in practice

personas.title: Data & Analytics Architect
signatureQuestions: Who owns this data forever?
Where is it cataloged and classified?
What happens to bad data here?
easyEAChecks: Is the data model as simple as it needs to be — not a maximalist capture of everything possible? (P6)
Are data insights connected to real decisions someone needs to make? (P1, P5)
Can data governance be maintained as a lightweight, everyday practice — not a separate compliance effort? (P7)

personas.title: State CIO
signatureQuestions: How does this improve the resident experience?
Is this something we should build for everyone, not just one agency?
Who owns this long-term? And how much does that cost?
easyEAChecks: Is this business-first — starting with resident or agency outcomes, not technology? (P1, P3)
Is value being delivered at every step — not just at the end of a long project? (P2)
Is the scope appropriate — are we solving a real problem without over-building? (P5, P6)

standard.bias: Resident impact focus — checks EasyEA principle alignment at strategic level
